devops_france_content.intro: Need a reliable cloud infrastructure without hiring a full-time DevOps engineer? Based in Montpellier, I work remotely across France to set up your CI/CD pipeline, manage cloud environments, and automate deployments.
devops_france_content.section_why_title: Why outsource your DevOps to a freelancer?
section_why_items: A senior freelance DevOps costs 2 to 4× less than a full-time employee, with no employer costs
Immediate expertise on production tools: GitHub Actions, Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, AWS
One-off project or monthly retainer — adapt as your needs evolve
devops_france_content.section_stack_title: What I set up for you
section_stack_items: CI/CD: GitHub Actions, GitLab CI — multi-environment pipelines (dev, staging, prod)
Containerization: Docker, Kubernetes — orchestration and auto-scaling
AWS Cloud: EC2, RDS, S3, ECS, Route 53, CloudFront — full management
IaC: Terraform for reproducible, versioned infrastructure
Monitoring: Prometheus, Grafana, Sentry — alerting and observability
section_process_items: Audit of your existing infrastructure (free, 30 min)
Architecture proposal and detailed quote within 48h

faq.question: How much does a freelance DevOps mission cost?
faq.answer: A senior freelance DevOps rate is between €400 and €700/day depending on complexity. I also offer monthly retainers (maintenance + monitoring) starting from €500/month.

faq.question: Do you work on-site in Montpellier?
faq.answer: Yes, I'm based in Montpellier and can work on-site in the Occitanie region. For remote projects, I work across all of France.

faq.question: How quickly can a CI/CD pipeline be set up?
faq.answer: A basic CI/CD pipeline (test → build → deploy to staging + production) can be up and running in 3 to 5 business days, depending on your existing stack.

creation_site_content.intro: A professional website isn't just about design — it needs to load fast, rank on Google, and convert visitors into clients. I build custom sites with Next.js, integrated SEO, and hosting included.
section_for_items: Freelancers and consultants who want a credible professional presence
SMEs whose current site no longer generates leads
Tradespeople and retailers who want to be found on Google locally
Startups that need a landing page or quick MVP
section_includes_items: Domain name registered in your name
Free SSL + performance optimization (Core Web Vitals)
Technical SEO: meta tags, sitemap, schema.org
creation_site_content.section_timeline_text: Showcase site: 2 to 3 weeks. Landing page: 1 week. E-commerce site: 4 to 6 weeks. These timelines include review rounds.

faq.question: How much does a professional website cost?
faq.answer: A custom showcase site starts from €1,500. The price depends on the number of pages, features, and level of customization. I provide a detailed quote within 48h.

faq.question: Do you create websites in Montpellier?
faq.answer: Yes, I'm based in Montpellier and work both in person and remotely. All projects meet the same quality standards regardless of location.

faq.question: Can I update my site myself after delivery?
faq.answer: Yes. Depending on your needs, I integrate a CMS (Sanity, Notion, or a simple solution) that lets you update text and images without touching the code.

app_web_content.intro: You have a web app idea but don't know where to start? I handle development end-to-end: architecture, backend, frontend, deployment and maintenance. Modern stack, clean code, fast delivery.
section_types_items: MVP to validate your idea before hiring a team
REST or GraphQL API for your mobile app
section_stack_items: Frontend: Next.js 15 (App Router, SSR/SSG, native SEO)
Backend: Python 3.12 + FastAPI — fast, typed, auto-documented
Database: PostgreSQL 16 — reliable, scalable
Auth: NextAuth.js or Supabase Auth
Deployment: CI/CD GitHub Actions → AWS or VPS
Testing: Pytest (backend) + Playwright (e2e)
section_process_items: Scoping: we define priority features together (MVP first)
Architecture: documented technical choices, no hidden debt
Iterative development: regular deliveries, fast feedback
Production launch: CI/CD, monitoring, technical documentation

faq.question: How long does it take to build a web application?
faq.answer: An MVP (core features) is typically delivered in 6 to 10 weeks. A more complex application takes 3 to 6 months. I deliver in iterations so you have something working quickly.

faq.question: Can I take over the codebase after delivery?
faq.answer: Absolutely. The code is clean, documented, and hosted in your own Git repository. I can train your technical team or handle maintenance if you prefer.

faq.question: Do you use cutting-edge or battle-tested technologies?
faq.answer: Both. Next.js, FastAPI and PostgreSQL are mature technologies with large communities. I don't use experimental libraries in production.

profile_section.title: Who am I?
profile_section.name: Zouhir Echarif El Idrissi El Kandri
profile_section.bio: Based in Montpellier (Hérault, Occitanie), I've been helping startups, SMEs and large enterprises across France — Paris, Lyon, Toulouse, Bordeaux, Marseille, Nantes — for over 5 years, working fully remote. Java Spring Boot, DevOps CI/CD, website creation: I deliver end-to-end with a single point of contact and transparent pricing.
profile_section.adaptability: I adapt to my clients' existing tech stack — Java, Python, Node.js or any technology already in place — to meet the need without imposing a rewrite.

java_spring_boot_content.intro: Need a senior Java Spring Boot developer without ESN delays? Based in Montpellier, I work remotely across France to build your microservices, REST APIs and Spring Boot applications — from MVP to production.
java_spring_boot_content.section_why_title: Why is it hard to find a senior Java Spring Boot freelancer quickly?
section_why_items: Senior Java Spring Boot profiles are scarce — ESNs lock them in long-term contracts
Freelance platforms are full of profiles claiming Java seniority without real production experience
Going direct gives you access to the actual expert, not a salesperson reselling a junior
section_stack_items: Spring Boot 3.x — microservices, REST/GraphQL APIs, unit and integration tests
Spring Cloud — Config Server, Eureka, Gateway, Circuit Breaker (Resilience4j)
Databases: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB — optimised JPA/Hibernate repositories
Security: Spring Security, OAuth2, JWT — robust authentication and authorisation
CI/CD: Maven/Gradle + GitHub Actions — automated test/build/deploy pipelines
Observability: Micrometer, Actuator, Prometheus, Grafana — production monitoring
section_process_items: Free scoping call (30 min) — I understand your architecture and constraints
Technical proposal and quote within 48h — clear scope, realistic timelines
Iterative development with regular deliveries — you see progress in real time
Delivery: clean code, tests, documentation, operational CI/CD pipeline

section_usecases_items.title: Startup / scale-up
section_usecases_items.desc: Your MVP is showing its limits. I migrate to Spring Boot with a scalable microservices architecture, without rewriting everything at once.

section_usecases_items.title: SME / mid-size company
section_usecases_items.desc: Your Java monolith is ageing. I progressively split it into Spring Boot microservices, API by API, without service interruption.

section_usecases_items.title: Enterprise IT
section_usecases_items.desc: Your in-house team is overloaded. I step in to deliver a specific module — SSO, integration API, refactoring.

section_usecases_items.title: Agencies & consultancies
section_usecases_items.desc: You need a senior Java expert urgently. I integrate with your team and deliver on time.

java_spring_boot_content.midcta_heading: Got a Java Spring Boot project?
java_spring_boot_content.midcta_sub: First conversation free. I respond within 24h, no commitment.
java_spring_boot_content.geo_text: I'm Zouhir, a Java Spring Boot freelance developer based in Montpellier (Herault, Occitanie). I work remotely across France — Paris, Lyon, Toulouse, Bordeaux, Marseille, Nantes, Nimes, Beziers — and on-site in the Occitanie region when needed. My clients are startups, SMEs, mid-size companies and large enterprises that need senior Java expertise quickly, without ESN delays and costs.

faq.question: What is the rate for a Java Spring Boot freelancer in France?
faq.answer: A senior Java Spring Boot freelancer charges between 450 and 700 EUR/day in France. The rate varies based on architecture complexity and mission duration. For missions of 3 months or more, a 10-15% discount is negotiable. I provide a detailed quote within 24h.

faq.question: How quickly can a Java Spring Boot mission start?
faq.answer: I'm typically available within 1 to 2 weeks. After an initial scoping call, I provide a technical proposal within 48h. A mission can start within 5 business days if the scope is clear.

faq.question: Java Spring Boot freelancer vs consultancy: what's the real difference?
faq.answer: With a consultancy, you pay a 30-50% margin on top of the consultant's actual rate. With a freelancer, you interact directly with the expert delivering the work. No account manager, no middleman, no surprise on who actually works on your project.

faq.question: Do you work remotely or only in Montpellier?
faq.answer: I work primarily remotely from Montpellier for clients across France — Paris, Lyon, Toulouse, Bordeaux, Nantes, Marseille. On-site engagements are possible in the Occitanie region.

faq.question: What deliverables are provided at the end of a Java Spring Boot mission?
faq.answer: At the end of every mission: versioned code in your Git repository, unit and integration tests, OpenAPI/Swagger technical documentation, working CI/CD pipeline, and deployment guide. You own all the code.

faq.question: Have you worked on monolith-to-microservices migrations?
faq.answer: Yes. I have led several migrations from Java monoliths to Spring Boot microservices architectures. The key is a progressive approach — Strangler Fig Pattern — to avoid interrupting the existing service.

faq.question: Can you work on an existing Spring Boot application?
faq.answer: Yes. I can take over an existing application to audit it, fix performance issues, add features, or improve test coverage. No need to start from scratch.

faq.question: Do you work with Agile / Scrum teams?
faq.answer: Yes. I adapt to your existing Agile rituals — sprint planning, daily standups, code reviews, retrospectives. I can also suggest a lightweight process if you do not have one yet.

tibco_content.intro: Need a freelance TIBCO consultant for an integration, migration or maintenance project? Based in Montpellier, I work remotely across France for TIBCO BusinessWorks, EMS and BWCE missions — SMEs, mid-size companies and large enterprises.
tibco_content.section_why_title: Why is it so hard to find a senior TIBCO consultant quickly?
section_why_items: TIBCO skills are rare — few senior profiles are available outside major consultancies
IT departments often wait 4 to 8 weeks to source a TIBCO consultant via a consultancy, with a 30-50% margin
Going direct with a freelance TIBCO consultant means starting within 2 weeks at the expert's real rate
section_stack_items: TIBCO BusinessWorks 5.x / 6.x — process development, optimisation and migration
TIBCO EMS — queue and topic configuration, bridges, performance and security
TIBCO BW Container Edition (BWCE) — cloud deployment on Kubernetes
TIBCO Spotfire — dashboards, analytics, data source connections
Middleware integration — JMS, REST, SOAP, FTP, database connectors
Technical documentation and knowledge transfer included
section_process_items: Analysis of your existing TIBCO context (free, 30 min)
Technical proposal with clear scope and realistic timelines within 48h
Iterative development or migration — validatable deliveries at each stage
Delivery: tested processes, documentation, configured deployment pipelines

section_usecases_items.title: BW 5.x to BWCE migration
section_usecases_items.desc: Your IT department wants to move to a cloud-native architecture. I analyse your existing BW processes and progressively migrate to BWCE on Kubernetes.

section_usecases_items.title: Maintenance and evolution
section_usecases_items.desc: Your team lacks TIBCO resources. I step in to maintain existing flows and develop new integrations.

section_usecases_items.title: Complex system integration
section_usecases_items.desc: You have an integration project between an ERP (SAP, Oracle) and business applications. I design TIBCO flows with error handling and recovery mechanisms.

section_usecases_items.title: IT in transition
section_usecases_items.desc: You are evaluating a migration to another platform (MuleSoft, Apache Camel). I audit your TIBCO flows and document the target architecture.

tibco_content.midcta_sub: First conversation free. I respond within 24h, no commitment.
tibco_content.geo_text: I'm Zouhir, a freelance TIBCO consultant based in Montpellier (Herault, Occitanie). I work remotely across France — Paris, Lyon, Toulouse, Bordeaux, Marseille, Nantes, Nimes, Beziers — and on-site in the Occitanie region when needed. My clients are SMEs, mid-size companies and large enterprises that need TIBCO expertise available quickly, without ESN delays and costs.

faq.question: What is the rate for a freelance TIBCO consultant in France?
faq.answer: An experienced freelance TIBCO consultant charges between 500 and 800 EUR/day in France. The rarity of these profiles and project complexity justify this positioning. A discount is possible for longer missions (3 months+). Transparent quote within 24h.

faq.question: How quickly can a TIBCO mission start?
faq.answer: I am available within 1 to 2 weeks. After an initial 30-minute call, I provide a mission proposal within 48h. For urgent situations, starting within 5 business days is possible.

faq.question: Freelance TIBCO consultant vs consultancy: what is the real difference?
faq.answer: A consultancy charges a 30-50% margin on the consultant's actual rate. With a freelancer, you sign directly with the expert delivering the mission — no salesperson, no middleman, transparent day rate.

faq.question: Do you work remotely or only in Montpellier?
faq.answer: I work primarily remotely from Montpellier for clients across France. On-site engagements are possible in Occitanie (Montpellier, Toulouse, Nimes) and occasionally in Paris or Lyon.

faq.question: What deliverables are provided at the end of a TIBCO mission?
faq.answer: At the end of a mission: documented TIBCO processes, integration architecture diagrams, deployment guide, monitoring and incident recovery procedures. Everything is delivered in your existing code repository or documentation space.

faq.question: Have you worked on TIBCO BW 5.x to BWCE migrations?
faq.answer: Yes. Migrating from TIBCO BusinessWorks 5.x to BWCE is one of the most frequent requests. It involves analysing existing flows, partial refactoring for Kubernetes, and a blue/green deployment strategy to minimise downtime.

faq.question: Can TIBCO BW 5.x and BWCE coexist during a migration?
faq.answer: Yes, that is the recommended strategy. Both versions coexist during the transition — new processes in BWCE, existing ones remain in BW 5.x until fully migrated. I size the coexistence strategy according to your context.

faq.question: Do you work on TIBCO Spotfire projects?
faq.answer: Yes. I work on designing Spotfire dashboards, connecting to data sources (SQL, REST, files), and optimising analytical performance.
